args.query: SELECT business_ref FROM review WHERE date LIKE '%2018%'

exec_args.sql: SELECT business_ref FROM review WHERE date LIKE '%2018%'

args.code:
import json
import ast

# Load business data
# The result from query_db is stored in a file if it was large
try:
    with open(locals()['var_function-call-9675852888409091933'], 'r') as f:
        businesses = json.load(f)
except TypeError:
    # If it's directly a list (small result), use it directly
    businesses = locals()['var_function-call-9675852888409091933']

# Load review data
reviews = locals()['var_function-call-14418585688152316040']

# Set of businesses reviewed in 2018
reviewed_businesses_refs = set(r['business_ref'] for r in reviews)

# Helper to check parking
def has_parking(attrs):
    if not attrs or attrs == "None":
        return False
    
    # Check BikeParking
    if attrs.get('BikeParking') == 'True':
        return True
        
    # Check BusinessParking
    bp_str = attrs.get('BusinessParking')
    if bp_str and bp_str != 'None':
        try:
            # Parse the stringified dict
            # It looks like "{'garage': False, ...}"
            # Use ast.literal_eval for safe parsing
            bp_dict = ast.literal_eval(bp_str)
            if isinstance(bp_dict, dict):
                # If any value is True
                if any(bp_dict.values()):
                    return True
        except:
            pass
            
    return False

# Filter businesses
parking_businesses_ids = set()
for b in businesses:
    if has_parking(b.get('attributes')):
        parking_businesses_ids.add(b['business_id'])

# Convert business_id to business_ref format for comparison
# businessid_X -> businessref_X
# Just replace 'id' with 'ref'
parking_businesses_refs = set(bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in parking_businesses_ids)

# Intersection
intersection = parking_businesses_refs.intersection(reviewed_businesses_refs)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(len(intersection))

var_function-call-3296668712133798812.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_function-call-3296668712133798812.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM review WHERE date LIKE '%2018%'

exec_args.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM review WHERE date LIKE '%2018%'

args.code:
import json
import ast

# Load business data from the large file
with open(locals()['var_function-call-18071743109309442878'], 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

# Load review data (list of dicts)
reviews = locals()['var_function-call-14418585688152316040']

reviewed_businesses_refs = set(r['business_ref'] for r in reviews)

def has_parking(attrs):
    if not attrs or attrs == "None":
        return False
    
    # Check BikeParking
    if attrs.get('BikeParking') == 'True':
        return True
        
    # Check BusinessParking
    bp_str = attrs.get('BusinessParking')
    if bp_str and bp_str != 'None':
        try:
            bp_dict = ast.literal_eval(bp_str)
            if isinstance(bp_dict, dict):
                if any(bp_dict.values()):
                    return True
        except:
            pass
    return False

parking_businesses_ids = set()
for b in businesses:
    if has_parking(b.get('attributes')):
        parking_businesses_ids.add(b['business_id'])

# Convert businessid_X to businessref_X
parking_businesses_refs = set(bid.replace('businessid_', 'businessref_') for bid in parking_businesses_ids)

# Intersection
intersection = parking_businesses_refs.intersection(reviewed_businesses_refs)

print("__RESULT__:")
print(len(intersection))

var_function-call-4488694828605818824.description: Located at 9655 E US Hwy 36, Unit H in Avon, IN, this establishment offers a range of services including Nail Salons, Hair Removal, Beauty & Spas, and Waxing.

var_function-call-3296668712133798812.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json
import ast

with open(locals()['var_function-call-18071743109309442878'], 'r') as f:
    businesses = json.load(f)

bp_values = set()
bike_values = set()

for b in businesses:
    attrs = b.get('attributes')
    if isinstance(attrs, dict):
        bp = attrs.get('BusinessParking')
        bike = attrs.get('BikeParking')
        bp_values.add(str(bp))
        bike_values.add(str(bike))

print("__RESULT__:")
print(json.dumps({
    "BusinessParking_samples": list(bp_values)[:10],
    "BikeParking_samples": list(bike_values)[:10]
}))

var_function-call-3296668712133798812.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

import ast

bike_values = set()

for b in businesses:

        bp = attrs.get('BusinessParking')
        bike = attrs.get('BikeParking')
        bp_values.add(str(bp))

print(json.dumps({
    "BusinessParking_samples": list(bp_values)[:10],
